highlights.text: As readers know, my favorite footprint is the Failed Breakdown and this is how institutions accumulate: They wait for price to put in a vicious, “elevator down” sell, undercut a low, trap shorts/grab stops on longs, then squeeze.
An elevator down sell to setup a Failed Breakdown.
This was an “elevator down” sell, meaning we prepare for the Failed Breakdown and short-squeeze.
I tweeted live at 9:45AM Tuesday that the 6605 Failed Breakdown would squeeze us up.
If not, wait for the Failed Breakdown of that 6667 low.” We hit 6652 low of day, recovered 6667, ripped into the close.
I was looking for this target all week, writing Monday at 4pm: “Targets are 6715-23 which tackles the red “megaphone” above yet again.
This back-test remains a magnet.” However, we would need a setup to rally us there.
I wrote yesterday: “The 6766 full backtest is a lower risk spot to short.” We held it exact, and down we went 100+ points to 6652 low of day.

post_title: Macro Rally Target Has Been Hit For SPX, and We Sold. Can Bulls Buy This Dip? Oct 16 Plan
